message.content: Adversaries may exploit open technical databases to gather information about potential victims, aiding in their targeting efforts. These databases can include domain and certificate registrations, as well as public collections of network data and artifacts from traffic or scans. By searching these resources, adversaries can uncover valuable details that facilitate further reconnaissance, such as phishing campaigns or probing open websites/domains. Additionally, the information can help in establishing operational resources, like acquiring or compromising infrastructure, and gaining initial access through methods such as exploiting external remote services or leveraging trusted relationships. Mitigation strategies include regularly monitoring and auditing public information about the organization, employing domain privacy services, and educating employees about the risks of oversharing technical details online.
